Global (All).title: US Congress Passes Bill to Increase Surveillance Powers
Global (All).summary: The US Congress has passed a bill that will increase the surveillance powers of law enforcement agencies, sparking concerns about privacy and civil liberties.

Global (All).title: Global Cyber Attack Hits Major Companies
Global (All).summary: A global cyber attack has hit several major companies, causing significant disruptions and raising concerns about cybersecurity and data protection.

Global (All).title: Protesters Clash with Police in Major City
Global (All).summary: Protesters clashed with police in a major city, leading to injuries and property damage, and highlighting tensions between law enforcement and marginalized communities.

United States.title: Tensions Rise Between US and China Over Pelosi's Taiwan Visit
US Supreme Court Rules in Favor of Religious Rights in LGBTQ+ Cases

South Korea.summary: 'Protest Against Labor Law Changes' held in South Korea.
'Rising North Korea concerns after missile test' has raised concerns about regional security

Japan.title: Mass protests erupt in Tokyo over COVID-19 measures
Japan.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets in Tokyo to protest against the Japanese government's plans to implement stricter COVID-19 measures, including mandatory vaccinations for all residents. The protests are a reflection of the growing tensions between the government and the general public over the issue of individual freedoms and the role of the state in public health policy.

Japan.title: Japan's parliament passes controversial constitutional security bill
Japan.summary: Japan's parliament has passed a contentious security bill that expands the role of the country's military and intelligence agencies. The bill has been criticized by opposition parties and human rights groups, who argue that it will erode civil liberties and give the government too much power.

Japan.title: Anti-Nuclear Protests Erupt in Japan
Japan.summary: Protesters have taken to the streets in cities across Japan to demand an end to the country's use of nuclear power. The protests follow a series of scandals and accidents at Japan's nuclear power plants, which have raised concerns about the safety of the country's energy policy.
